title: The Straw Hats Watch the Giants
description: On pages 9–12, the straw hats watch the giants as the giants’ proud battle becomes the center of the island as the Straw Hats learn what Elbaf honor means.
scene: The Straw Hats Watch the Giants. The giants’ proud battle becomes the center of the island as the Straw Hats learn what Elbaf honor means. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And it was just too amusing to observe,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: A single princess who foolishly thinks she can take on the entire baroque works organization and save her kingdom...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: and may we meet again in our home- land.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Do you under- stand what's going on...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But what I do know for sure,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Is that this woman's a threat to my lovely Miss Wednesday...!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Not pointing such dangerous weapons at me?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Fufufu... no need to get so riled up.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I haven't come here on any orders nor do I have any reasons for fighting you people.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you're the captain of the straw-hat pirates,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Patients are not allowed to be able to

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Gimme back my hat! So you are trying to pick a fight with us, are ya now!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I've now decided to see you as my enemy so come at me!!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: How unfortunate... Small-time pirates having to look after a Princess targeted by the entire Baroque works organization...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And a lone princess having to entrust herself to a few small- time pirates...

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the straw hats watch the giants. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
